author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

Measured wall-clock at three sizes (`experiments/scale-index/`):

| N | `list_ready` (today) | `resolve` slug worst | wc-walk rebuild | cache hit |
|---|-----:|-----:|-----:|-----:|
| 17 | 0.38s | 0.19s | 0.09s | 0.014s |
| 100 | 2.23s | 1.10s | 0.14s | 0.014s |
| 1000 | 22.5s | 11.2s | 0.60s | 0.015s |
| 10000 | ~225s (extrap) | ~110s (extrap) | 5.1s | 0.025s |

Per-issue cost in today's `list_ready` is ~22 ms — matches the
~15 ms per-`jj`-invocation floor pinned in
`experiments/jj-cli-overhead/README.md`, times two `jj file show`
spawns (`.json` + `.comments.jsonl`).

The cache-hit column is the decisive datum: bounded at ~15-25 ms
through N=10000, dominated by the head-commit probe + a single
JSON parse. A snapshot cache by itself crushes the read path
through the largest scale we care about.

A real index (rusqlite, redb) buys nothing extra at this scale.

# Recommendation

**Ship the snapshot cache; do NOT ship a real index.** Pin the
rusqlite-bundled index design as a fallback if rebuild costs
ever start to dominate.

Design doc: `docs/storage-index-design.md` (six sections per the
ticket's acceptance criteria).

Follow-up ticket filed: `61e9a1c` — `storage-snapshot-cache`,
type `feature`, slug `storage-snapshot-cache`, labeled
`epic:mvp-storage`. No second ticket for an index: at the
measured scale, the cache alone clears the bar.

# Confidence

High. Three signals:

1. The benchmark is a real one (not estimated): wall-clock on
   synthetic bookmarks at the exact sizes the ticket called for.
2. The cache architecture is the simplest possible thing that
   could work — single file in `.jj/`, head-commit probe,
   working-copy walk on miss. Familiar pattern; few moving
   parts.
3. The index fallback is fully designed (`docs/storage-index-design.md`
   §3-§5). If the cache ever proves insufficient, we know
   exactly which library and schema to reach for. No design
   rework required.

# Open follow-ups

- Implement `61e9a1c` (the cache).
- If/when the cache ever needs an index sitting underneath it
  (no measured signal today), file the rusqlite implementation
  ticket against `docs/storage-index-design.md` §3-§5.
